evidence.causal_reasoning: After #9323, this became user-visible because `didChangeConfiguration` started reloading workspace settings immediately, causing the misread settings to re-enable capabilities/diagnostics unexpectedly

title: feat(biome_html_analyze): port useKeyWithMouseEvents a11y rule to HTML
author: rahuld109

evidence.lived_experience: Author discloses skill level ('I have experience with web accessibility standards, I am still learning Rust') but does not narrate a lived problem — this is skill disclosure, not experience narrative
evidence.organizational_memory: References Biome's existing JSX rule behavior, issue #8155, and states: 'The existing Biome JSX rule does not implement these extended options — it only checks the default pair. This HTML port matches the current Biome JSX behavior'
evidence.uncertainty: 'I am still learning Rust' — explicit acknowledgment of skill gap; AI Assistance Disclosure signals transparency about tool usage rather than trying to hide it
evidence.causal_reasoning: 'In HTML, attribute names are matched case-insensitively. In component-based frameworks (Vue, Svelte, Astro), PascalCase elements are assumed to be custom components and are ignored' — grounded in HTML/framework specs, not generic best practice
evidence.cognitive_questions: No genuine open questions; AI Disclosure and scope notes are statements, not inquiries
